subject: Cryptography
title: Elliptic Curve Encryption
description: Elliptic Curve Encryption (ECC) is a form of public-key cryptography based on the algebraic structure of elliptic curves over finite fields. ECC offers the same level of security as traditional methods like RSA but with smaller key sizes, making it more efficient. In this lesson, we will explore the mathematics behind ECC and its applications in securing communications.
objectives: Understand the mathematical principles behind elliptic curves
Learn how ECC keys are generated and managed
Explore the advantages of ECC over traditional encryption methods
prerequisites: Understanding of basic elliptic curve properties
